verses.text: At Ephesus Paul finds disciples; 'Have ye received the Holy Ghost?' 'We have not... heard.' Incomplete faith. They knew little of the Spirit.
At Ephesus Paul finds disciples; 'Have ye received the Holy Ghost?' 'We have not... heard.' Incomplete faith. They knew little of the Spirit.
Baptized into John's baptism, they are now baptized 'in the name of the Lord Jesus.' Brought further. They move from John's preparation to full faith.
Baptized into John's baptism, they are now baptized 'in the name of the Lord Jesus.' Brought further. They move from John's preparation to full faith.
Baptized into John's baptism, they are now baptized 'in the name of the Lord Jesus.' Brought further. They move from John's preparation to full faith.
He lays hands on them; the Holy Ghost comes, and they speak with tongues. The Spirit given. God seals their fuller faith.
He lays hands on them; the Holy Ghost comes, and they speak with tongues. The Spirit given. God seals their fuller faith.
He disputes in the school of Tyrannus two years, so 'all... in Asia heard the word.' Wide reach. Steady teaching spreads the gospel far.
He disputes in the school of Tyrannus two years, so 'all... in Asia heard the word.' Wide reach. Steady teaching spreads the gospel far.
He disputes in the school of Tyrannus two years, so 'all... in Asia heard the word.' Wide reach. Steady teaching spreads the gospel far.
God works special miracles; even handkerchiefs from Paul heal the sick. Mighty signs. God honours the gospel at Ephesus.
God works special miracles; even handkerchiefs from Paul heal the sick. Mighty signs. God honours the gospel at Ephesus.
Vagabond exorcists use the name; the spirit says, 'Jesus I know... but who are ye?' and overcomes them. No empty charm. Christ's name is no spell for the unbelieving.
Vagabond exorcists use the name; the spirit says, 'Jesus I know... but who are ye?' and overcomes them. No empty charm. Christ's name is no spell for the unbelieving.
Vagabond exorcists use the name; the spirit says, 'Jesus I know... but who are ye?' and overcomes them. No empty charm. Christ's name is no spell for the unbelieving.
Vagabond exorcists use the name; the spirit says, 'Jesus I know... but who are ye?' and overcomes them. No empty charm. Christ's name is no spell for the unbelieving.
Fear falls; many burn their books of magic; 'so mightily grew the word.' True repentance. They forsake their old sins openly.
Fear falls; many burn their books of magic; 'so mightily grew the word.' True repentance. They forsake their old sins openly.
Fear falls; many burn their books of magic; 'so mightily grew the word.' True repentance. They forsake their old sins openly.
Fear falls; many burn their books of magic; 'so mightily grew the word.' True repentance. They forsake their old sins openly.
Paul purposes Jerusalem and Rome, sending Timothy ahead. Forward look. He plans ever to carry the gospel further.
Paul purposes Jerusalem and Rome, sending Timothy ahead. Forward look. He plans ever to carry the gospel further.
Demetrius the silversmith stirs the craftsmen, fearing for their trade in Diana's shrines. Threatened gain. The gospel hurts the idol-makers' profit.
Demetrius the silversmith stirs the craftsmen, fearing for their trade in Diana's shrines. Threatened gain. The gospel hurts the idol-makers' profit.
Demetrius the silversmith stirs the craftsmen, fearing for their trade in Diana's shrines. Threatened gain. The gospel hurts the idol-makers' profit.
Demetrius the silversmith stirs the craftsmen, fearing for their trade in Diana's shrines. Threatened gain. The gospel hurts the idol-makers' profit.
Demetrius the silversmith stirs the craftsmen, fearing for their trade in Diana's shrines. Threatened gain. The gospel hurts the idol-makers' profit.
They cry, 'Great is Diana of the Ephesians,' filling the city with confusion. Mob roused. Self-interest masquerades as zeal for the goddess.
They cry, 'Great is Diana of the Ephesians,' filling the city with confusion. Mob roused. Self-interest masquerades as zeal for the goddess.
They cry, 'Great is Diana of the Ephesians,' filling the city with confusion. Mob roused. Self-interest masquerades as zeal for the goddess.
They cry, 'Great is Diana of the Ephesians,' filling the city with confusion. Mob roused. Self-interest masquerades as zeal for the goddess.
The assembly is confused; for two hours they shout, 'Great is Diana.' Senseless uproar. The crowd knows not why it gathered.
The assembly is confused; for two hours they shout, 'Great is Diana.' Senseless uproar. The crowd knows not why it gathered.
The assembly is confused; for two hours they shout, 'Great is Diana.' Senseless uproar. The crowd knows not why it gathered.
The townclerk quiets them; these men are no robbers of churches nor blasphemers. Calm reason. A pagan official defends the apostles' innocence.
The townclerk quiets them; these men are no robbers of churches nor blasphemers. Calm reason. A pagan official defends the apostles' innocence.
The townclerk quiets them; these men are no robbers of churches nor blasphemers. Calm reason. A pagan official defends the apostles' innocence.
If Demetrius has a matter, the law is open; he dismisses the assembly. Order restored. The riot ends without harm to God's servants.
